title: ESSA: Evolutionary Strategies for Scalable Alignment
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: This paper proposes ESSA (Evolutionary Strategies for Scalable Alignment), a framework for aligning Large Language Models using Evolutionary Strategies. The approach addresses limitations of RLHF and its variants by offering high parallelizability, efficient scaling, robustness with sparse rewards, and reduced memory requirements.

key_techniques: Adaptation of LoRA technique to the ES paradigm

contributions: Adaptation of LoRA technique to the ES paradigm
Demonstration of faster convergence and better data efficiency compared to GRPO
Demonstration of efficient scaling across distributed compute environments and avoidance of memory bottlenecks
advantages: Scales efficiently across many computing nodes
Requires less memory by operating in inference-only mode without gradient computations
related_work: Online alignment methods like RLOO and GRPO
